register: Reviewed lesson-source excerpts establish a specific counterpossibility for the named lesson action. A registry-derived candidate does not enter this file until a local source excerpt supports the lesson-level attribution.

receipts.material_incompatibility: The response leaves part of the available sandwich undistributed, while equal sharing requires the full available quantity to be partitioned among the people.

fragments.text: There may be fewer people than sandwiches
students only share whole sandwiches
people share 5 sandwiches and each gets 2
If students ignore the extra sandwich

receipts.material_incompatibility: Comparing 62 with 1 without coordinating centimeters and meters conflicts with the conversion relation that one meter is one hundred centimeters.

fragments.text: Students may say that 62 centimeters is longer than 1
meter because 62 is greater than 1.

receipts.material_incompatibility: The response exchanges the number of groups with the number in each group, so the representation no longer preserves the roles named in the situation.

fragments.text: Students indicate that they mixed up the number of
groups and the number of objects in each group by
making 5 groups of 3.

receipts.material_incompatibility: Representing six bags rather than six apples in each bag exchanges the sought number of groups with the divisor that fixes each group size.

fragments.text: Students represent 6 bags of apples instead of 6 apples in
each bag.

receipts.material_incompatibility: Naming only the opposite equal side leaves two boundary segments unaccounted for, so it cannot determine the rectangle's complete perimeter.

fragments.text: Students identify the side that is 32 feet long
but not the other two sides of the
swimming pool.

receipts.material_incompatibility: An expression whose factors do not represent the array's rows and objects per row fails to preserve the represented equal-groups structure.

fragments.text: Students write a multiplication expression that does not
match the array they created

receipts.material_incompatibility: Assigning different unknown values to equations that represent the same situation breaks the multiplication-division relation the paired equations retain.

fragments.text: Students say that different numbers should be used to
complete each equation.

receipts.material_incompatibility: A difference-unknown calculation answers how many more, while the task asks for the greater quantity produced by combining the smaller quantity and the difference.

fragments.text: Students find the value of the difference between 17 and
rather than the value of the sum
of 17 and 9 (greater unknown).

receipts.material_incompatibility: Adding the new seeds to one earlier addend omits the other part of the prior total and therefore does not preserve the composed quantity being updated.

fragments.text: Students add the 38 new seeds in the second problem to
only one addend from the first problem.

receipts.material_incompatibility: Stopping after one pair of addends does not satisfy the lesson's demand to find multiple compositions of ten and test whether the set is complete.

fragments.text: Students do not yet find more than one solution to the
problem.
